description_long: This house features a traditional design with a prominent gable roof covered in weathered, dark gray tiles, showing signs of moss and age. The exterior walls are a mix of white plaster and exposed dark wooden beams, giving it a classic timber-framed appearance. The base of the house is lined with stonework, and there are visible patches of green moss along the lower edges, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. Windows are rectangular with black grilles, and the front includes a small covered entryway with a tiled awning supported by wooden posts. The structure is single-story, with a simple, functional layout and minimal decorative elements. The overall look is reminiscent of traditional Japanese or East Asian rural homes, blending natural materials and practical design.
description: A single-story, timber-framed house with a weathered gable roof, white plaster walls, and a small covered entryway. The exterior features dark wooden beams, stonework at the base, and rectangular windows with black grilles.
description_view_0: Top-down view highlighting the large, weathered gable roof and the small covered entryway extending from the main structure.
description_view_1: Front view showing the main entryway with a tiled awning, a large window with black grilles, and the timber-framed facade.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the long stretch of the gable roof, two windows (one with a grid pattern), and the stone-lined base.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the plain white plaster wall with exposed beams and no visible windows or doors.
attribute_entryway_design: Small covered entryway with a tiled awning, wooden posts, and a single main door.
